description: Agent value benchmark: does an agent with watercooler tools succeed at tasks that an agent without them fails? Both agents get the watercooler-site codebase. The tools agent additionally gets the real thread history from the orphan branch.
project_context:
You are working on 'watercooler-site', a Next.js web dashboard for collaborative agentic coding. Tech stack: Next.js 14 + TypeScript, Vercel (deployment), Neon PostgreSQL + Prisma ORM (database), Slack integration (Block Kit, OAuth), GitHub OAuth for authentication, watercooler protocol for threaded project history.

If you have access to project knowledge tools (watercooler_search, watercooler_list_threads, watercooler_read_thread, watercooler_list_thread_entries, watercooler_get_thread_entry), follow this search workflow:
1. Start with watercooler_search using SHORT keyword queries (1-3 words). Example: query="long polling" NOT query="what notification mechanism was chosen for the dashboard". Queries are tokenized by whitespace and ALL tokens must appear in the entry.
2. Use watercooler_list_threads to discover available thread topics.
3. Use watercooler_list_thread_entries(topic=..., format="json") to browse entry summaries within a thread.
4. Use watercooler_get_thread_entry(topic=..., index=N) to read the full body of a specific entry.
5. For cross-thread tasks, search each concept separately (e.g. search "advisory lock" then search "deployment"), then synthesize.

If tools are not available, do your best based on available context.

tasks.title: Recall the chosen notification mechanism
tasks.problem_statement:
The team previously discussed and decided on a real-time notification mechanism for the dashboard.

Write a file called DECISION.md that states:
1. Which mechanism was chosen (Pusher, WebSockets, long polling, or SSE)
2. The specific endpoint path that was decided on
3. The polling interval in seconds
4. Which alternatives were rejected and why

Do NOT guess - find the decision that was already made.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'long polling' DECISION.md && grep -q '/api/dashboard/wait-for-update' DECISION.md && grep -q '1.5' DECISION.md

tasks.title: Recall the production testing policy
tasks.problem_statement:
The team has an established policy about testing in production environments and branch-to-environment mapping.

Write a file called POLICY.md that states:
1. The policy on testing in production
2. The complete branch-to-environment mapping (which branches deploy where)
3. Which deployment methods are allowed and which are forbidden

Do NOT make up a policy - find the one that was already established.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'never test in production\|do not test in production' POLICY.md && grep -qi 'stable.*production' POLICY.md && grep -qi 'main.*preview' POLICY.md

tasks.title: Recall the stdio-to-HTTP bridging approach
tasks.problem_statement:
The team evaluated three approaches to bridge the gap between the local stdio MCP mode and the hosted HTTP mode. A decision was made.

Write a file called ARCHITECTURE.md that states:
1. Which approach was selected (Full GitHub API Migration, Context Propagation with Mode Detection, or Server-Side Clone)
2. Why it was selected over the alternatives
3. The key design elements of the chosen approach

Do NOT guess - find the architectural decision that was already made.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'context propagation' ARCHITECTURE.md && grep -qi 'mode detection' ARCHITECTURE.md && grep -qi 'contextvars\|context variable' ARCHITECTURE.md

tasks.title: Recall the P3009 migration recovery procedure
tasks.problem_statement:
There is an established pattern for recovering from Prisma P3009 'failed migration' errors in the production database.

Write a file called RECOVERY.md that documents:
1. What causes a P3009 error
2. How to diagnose it (what SQL queries to run)
3. The exact fix pattern (the SQL UPDATE statement)

Do NOT guess - find the established recovery pattern.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'P3009' RECOVERY.md && grep -qi '_prisma_migrations' RECOVERY.md && grep -qi 'finished_at' RECOVERY.md

tasks.title: Recall why dashboard shows 'No threads found'
tasks.problem_statement:
Users reported that the dashboard shows 'No threads found' after connecting a repository. The root cause was diagnosed.

Write a file called DIAGNOSIS.md that explains:
1. The root cause of the 'No threads found' issue
2. Which step in the data flow is broken
3. What the user has to do manually as a workaround

Do NOT guess - find the diagnosis that was already made.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'automatic sync\|auto.*sync\|sync.*automatic' DIAGNOSIS.md && grep -qi 'manually' DIAGNOSIS.md && grep -qi 'connect' DIAGNOSIS.md

tasks.title: Summarize the Neon transfer spike incident
tasks.problem_statement:
There was a major database transfer spike incident with the Neon PostgreSQL database that threatened to exhaust the 5GB transfer limit.

Write a file called INCIDENT_SUMMARY.md summarizing:
1. The root cause of the transfer spike (what specific operation caused it)
2. The specific data volume involved
3. What optimization phases were applied
4. The before and after transfer rates (MB/day)
5. How many days the 5GB limit will now last

Do NOT speculate - find the actual incident details from project records.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi '3.37\|3\.37' INCIDENT_SUMMARY.md && grep -qi '21.5\|21\.5' INCIDENT_SUMMARY.md && grep -qi '233' INCIDENT_SUMMARY.md

tasks.title: Report current state of Slack integration
tasks.problem_statement:
You need to write a status report on the Slack integration work for a stakeholder check-in.

Write a file called SLACK_STATUS.md that describes:
1. Which implementation phases are complete (list each phase and status)
2. What is currently being worked on (active work)
3. Any known debugging issues or open bugs

Do NOT speculate - find the actual project status from records.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'phase 1.*complete\|phase 1.*done' SLACK_STATUS.md && grep -qi 'phase 5\|legacy auth' SLACK_STATUS.md && grep -qi 'view_submission\|modal' SLACK_STATUS.md

tasks.title: Summarize the OAuth redirect failure root cause
tasks.problem_statement:
The NextAuth OAuth login flow had a critical failure that was diagnosed and fixed.

Write a file called OAUTH_POSTMORTEM.md that explains:
1. What error the user saw (the symptom)
2. The root cause (what API mismatch caused it)
3. What specific code changes fixed it
4. Any remaining cleanup work

Do NOT speculate - find the actual postmortem from project records.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'v4.*v5\|v5.*v4\|NextAuth' OAUTH_POSTMORTEM.md && grep -qi 'callbackUrl\|redirectTo\|UnknownAction' OAUTH_POSTMORTEM.md && grep -qi '189\|workaround\|cleanup' OAUTH_POSTMORTEM.md

tasks.title: Summarize sync performance optimizations
tasks.problem_statement:
Multiple phases of sync performance optimization were completed. You need to document the results.

Write a file called PERF_REPORT.md that covers:
1. What optimizations were made (list each phase)
2. The before and after performance numbers
3. How the rate limit issue was fixed
4. The specific technique used for change detection

Do NOT speculate - find the actual optimization results from project records.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'incremental' PERF_REPORT.md && grep -qi 'SHA\|sha' PERF_REPORT.md && grep -qi '60.*1\|less than 1' PERF_REPORT.md && grep -qi '450\|purge' PERF_REPORT.md

tasks.title: Identify the CORRECTED root cause (not the initial wrong one)
tasks.problem_statement:
There was a Prisma migration failure. The initial analysis was later CORRECTED with a more accurate root cause.

Write a file called ROOT_CAUSE.md with:
1. The CORRECT (most recent) root cause of the migration failure
2. What the INCORRECT earlier analysis said (so we don't repeat the mistake)
3. Why the earlier analysis was wrong

IMPORTANT: There are two analyses - an initial one and a corrected one. You MUST identify and use the CORRECTED root cause, not the initial one. The initial analysis blamed 'workflow deviation' but that was wrong.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'index.*constraint\|constraint.*mismatch\|pg_indexes.*pg_constraint' ROOT_CAUSE.md && grep -qi 'workflow deviation' ROOT_CAUSE.md && grep -qi 'incorrect\|wrong\|supersede\|corrected\|not caused' ROOT_CAUSE.md

tasks.title: Describe current (not old) dashboard data access approach
tasks.problem_statement:
The way the dashboard accesses thread data has changed over time. An old approach was replaced with a new one.

Write a file called DATA_ACCESS.md with:
1. The CURRENT approach for how the dashboard accesses thread data
2. What the OLD approach was (that was replaced)
3. What database table was removed in the transition

IMPORTANT: Make sure you describe the CURRENT approach, not the old one. The old approach used a database table; the new one reads directly from files.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'baseline graph' DATA_ACCESS.md && grep -qi 'ThreadSnapshot.*removed\|removed.*ThreadSnapshot' DATA_ACCESS.md && grep -qi 'current\|now' DATA_ACCESS.md

tasks.title: Describe current (not old) branch storage model
tasks.problem_statement:
The thread storage architecture's handling of branches has changed. An old model was replaced.

Write a file called BRANCH_MODEL.md with:
1. The CURRENT model for how branches relate to thread storage
2. What the OLD model was (that was replaced)
3. How entries are associated with branches now

IMPORTANT: The old model used separate repos/branches as storage dimensions. The new model is different. Describe the CURRENT one.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'code_branch' BRANCH_MODEL.md && grep -qi 'orphan' BRANCH_MODEL.md && grep -qi 'filter\|tag\|metadata' BRANCH_MODEL.md

tasks.title: Synthesize deploy + migration failure scenarios from two threads
tasks.problem_statement:
When Vercel deployments trigger Prisma migrations, failures can occur from multiple causes.

Write a file called DEPLOY_FAILURES.md that covers:
1. What happens when a deployment triggers a P1002 advisory lock timeout (symptoms, resolution)
2. What happens when a deployment triggers a P3009 failed migration (the specific migration name, root cause, SQL fix)
3. The recovery procedure for each

Both failure modes have been encountered on this project. Find the specific details — don't write generic Prisma documentation.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'P1002' DEPLOY_FAILURES.md && grep -qi 'P3009' DEPLOY_FAILURES.md && grep -qi 'redeploy' DEPLOY_FAILURES.md && grep -qi '20260113.*add_graph_columns\|add_graph_columns' DEPLOY_FAILURES.md

tasks.title: Analyze Slack rebuild interaction with sync rate limits
tasks.problem_statement:
The Slack integration has a rebuild feature, and there have been sync performance issues including rate limiting.

Write a file called REBUILD_ANALYSIS.md that covers:
1. What the Slack rebuild operation does and its known issues (collision protection, dual implementation)
2. How the sync system was optimized to handle rate limits (what specific fix was applied)
3. Whether a rebuild could trigger rate limit exhaustion and why/why not

Both topics have been worked on in this project. Find the specific implementation details and numbers — don't speculate.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'collision\|locking\|no.*protection' REBUILD_ANALYSIS.md && grep -qi 'purge.*true\|450.*per entry\|rate limit' REBUILD_ANALYSIS.md && grep -qi 'incremental\|SHA' REBUILD_ANALYSIS.md

tasks.title: Synthesize authentication and onboarding flow from two threads
tasks.problem_statement:
New users go through an authentication and onboarding flow that spans OAuth login and repository setup. Issues were found and fixed across both areas.

Write a file called ONBOARDING.md that covers:
1. What the OAuth authentication failure was and how it was fixed
2. What onboarding improvements were deployed (P0 and P1 phases)
3. Known remaining issues or workarounds

Both areas have been actively worked on in this project. Find the specific issues and fixes — don't speculate.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'OAuth\|NextAuth' ONBOARDING.md && grep -qi 'HTTPS\|GIT_ASKPASS' ONBOARDING.md && grep -qi 'multi.org\|UserOrganization' ONBOARDING.md

tasks.title: Explain how per-thread format enables efficient writes
tasks.problem_statement:
The graph storage format was migrated from monolithic to per-thread, which enabled a new tiered write architecture.

Write a file called WRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md that covers:
1. What changed in the graph format (old monolithic vs new per-thread structure)
2. How the per-thread format enables efficient metadata updates
3. What specific file is updated for status/ball/priority changes
4. Any limitations of the new approach

Both changes have been made in this project. Find the specific design decisions and limitations — don't speculate.
tasks.test_cmd: grep -qi 'meta.json' WRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md && grep -qi 'per.thread' WRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md && grep -qi 'status\|ball\|priority' WRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md && grep -qi '501\|rename\|limitation' WRITE_ARCHITECTURE.md
